---
title: "Russ Zaliznyak"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::::: {.home-layout}

:::: {.profile-sidebar}

![](profile.jpg){.profile-photo}

[Russ Zaliznyak]{.profile-name}

[Principal Data Scientist]{.profile-role}

::: {.topic-badges}
[AB Testing]{.badge-category} [Python]{.badge-category} [Statistics]{.badge-category} [Math]{.badge-category}
:::

[Spent 10 years shipping experiments. I write about the things that are easy to get subtly wrong.]{.profile-bio}

::: {.profile-links}
<i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i> [Email](mailto:rzaliznyak@gmail.com)

<i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/russ-zaliznyak/){target="_blank"}

<i class="bi bi-github"></i> [GitHub](https://github.com/rzaliznyak-math){target="_blank"}

<i class="bi bi-file-person-fill"></i> [CV](cv.html)
:::

::: {.sidebar-nav}
[Writing]{.sidebar-nav-label}

[AB Testing](ab-testing/index.html)

[Python](python/index.html)

[Talks & Videos]{.sidebar-nav-label}

[All Talks](talks/index.html)

[Going Bayes](https://share.vidyard.com/watch/wqUpv9CUAXj9cSrFzAQwQ3){target="_blank"}

[Intuit × Dash Enterprise](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IpcTF0UAIVQ){target="_blank"}
:::

::::

:::: {.article-list}

## The Winner's Curse

::: {.post-meta}
Jan 20, 2026 · AB Testing · Bias · Bayesian
:::

Significance tells you an effect exists — not how large it is. When only extreme results
cross the significance threshold, reported effect sizes are systematically inflated.
In an 80%-powered experiment, 62.5% of significant results overestimate the true lift.
Two fixes: run a bigger test, or shrink estimates toward a prior.

[Read →](ab-testing/winners-curse.qmd)

---

## Beta vs Binomial Distribution

::: {.post-meta}
Dec 9, 2025 · Statistics · Bayesian · Distributions
:::

Both distributions arise from the same relationship — one models outcomes given a probability,
the other models the probability given outcomes. Analyzes the same experiment both ways:
Binomial for p-values, Beta for credible intervals. Extends to two-proportion tests and
shows both are well-approximated by the Normal under large samples.

[Read →](ab-testing/beta-vs-binomial.qmd)

---

## Why Switch to Polars from Pandas

::: {.post-meta}
Oct 21, 2025 · Python · Performance
:::

Benchmarking Polars vs Pandas across common DataFrame operations: read, write, construct,
pivot, join, and filter. Polars wins across the board — often by 10–30x.

[Read →](python/polars-vs-pandas.qmd)

---

## The Value of Big Swings

::: {.post-meta}
Aug 29, 2025 · AB Testing · Strategy · Simulation
:::

Why bold experiments deliver more value with less traffic. Compares guaranteed small wins
vs. high-variance transformational bets using 30,000 simulations.

[Read →](ab-testing/big-swings.qmd)

---

## Experiment Design & Analysis with NumPy

::: {.post-meta}
Feb 8, 2025 · AB Testing · Simulation · Power
:::

Understanding p-values and statistical power through simulation. Builds a full AB test
framework from scratch using `numpy.random`.

[Read →](ab-testing/numpy-experiment-design.qmd)

---

## The Power of Data Simulation

::: {.post-meta}
Nov 4, 2024 · Simulation · NumPy · Intuition
:::

Using Python's NumPy random generator to model randomness and build intuition for
rate-based metrics. A foundation for all simulation-based work.

[Read →](ab-testing/power-of-simulation.qmd)

---

# Talks & Videos

## Going Bayes: Shifting Our Testing Methods To Reflect Our Priorities

::: {.post-meta}
Nov 6, 2025 · Intuit Tech Stories · Bayesian · Experimentation
:::

How aligning business priorities with Bayesian Risk-Based Testing reduced Intuit's experiment
duration by over 60%. Covers the tradeoffs of moving away from frequentist defaults and
scaling adoption across data science, product, and leadership.

[Watch →](https://share.vidyard.com/watch/wqUpv9CUAXj9cSrFzAQwQ3){target="_blank"}

---

## How Intuit Uses Dash Enterprise

::: {.post-meta}
Mar 2024 · Plotly · Dash · Experimentation Tooling
:::

Two-minute highlight from a Plotly webinar on using Dash Enterprise to build an
end-to-end experimentation analysis app — no HTML or engineering experience required.
Reduced experiment runtime by 70%+.

[Watch →](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IpcTF0UAIVQ){target="_blank"}

::::

:::::
